title: fatty british nectarine crumble
description: A sweet and buttery crumble dish featuring nectarines and a hint of spice, perfect for a cold winter evening. Note: this recipe is designed to be macrobiotic-friendly with wholesome, natural ingredients.

ingredients.name: nectarines
ingredients.notes: firm but not hard, about 1.5 pounds total

instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
In a mortar and pestle, pound 1/2 cup of almonds until finely ground, set aside.
In a large bowl, mix together 2 cups of rolled oats, 1 cup of brown sugar, and the ground almonds.
Add 1/2 cup of cold unsalted butter to the dry ingredients and use your fingers or a pastry blender to work it in until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Transfer the crumb mixture to a 9x9-inch baking dish and set aside.
Cut the nectarines into 1-inch pieces and place them in a separate 9x9-inch baking dish.
In a small bowl, mix together 1 tablespoon of honey, 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, and 1/4 teaspoon of ground cinnamon. Pour the honey-lemon mixture over the nectarines and toss to coat.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and top the nectarines with the crumb mixture, spreading it evenly to cover the fruit.
Return the baking dish to the oven and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the fruit is tender.
Let the crumble cool for a few minutes before serving, enjoying warm if possible.
